channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌍 A-10 Warthogs Arrive In England Festooned With Nose Art And Mission Markings From Epic Fury | The War Zone
item.description: The markings on the A-10s seen at RAF Lakenheath today offer an indication of what they did during the fight against Iran. The post A-10 Warthogs Arrive In England Festooned With Nose Art And Mission Markings From Epic Fury appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week | The War Zone
item.description: Everyday is like Sunday... The post Bunker Talk: Let’s Talk About All The Things We Did And Didn’t Cover This Week appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Only 1 in 4 F-35s is fully mission capable, GAO finds | Defense News
item.description: The F-35’s readiness rates continued to decline through fiscal 2025, with the fleet’s full mission capable rate falling to 25%, according to a GAO report.

item.title: 🕵️ A renewed security and cooperation agenda for Colombia’s next government | Atlantic Council
item.description: As Colombia heads to its presidential runoff election June 21, the next government will inherit a security crisis that requires a renewed US-Colombia agenda for dismantling criminal governance structures and the financial networks that sustain them. The post A renewed security…

item.title: 🌍 Netanyahu’s Reelection Could Hinge on Outcome of Iran War | Foreign Policy
item.description: He promised Israelis “total victory” but has fallen short on three fronts.

item.title: 🏛️ Boeing Drops Out Of Navy’s T-45 Jet Trainer Replacement Competition | The War Zone
item.description: The field of competitors to design and build the Navy's next jet trainer has narrowed to just two teams, and both are putting forward twin-engine designs. The post Boeing Drops Out Of Navy’s T-45 Jet Trainer Replacement Competition appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 It’s not a ‘deal.’ But Trump’s memorandum with Iran can be the start of something bigger. | Atlantic Council
item.description: What appears to be emerging now is a rough outline and high-level explanation of the concepts that an eventual deal could include. The post It’s not a ‘deal.’ But Trump’s memorandum with Iran can be the start of something bigger. appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Trump’s Big Weekend Could Derail the G-7 | Foreign Policy
item.description: With or without a deal, the Iran war looms large over the three-day leaders’ summit.

item.title: 📍 How South Africa’s Myopia Is Harming the Entire Continent | Foreign Policy
item.description: Xenophobia in Africa’s southernmost nation will have vast implications.

item.title: 🏛️ The United States needs more amphibious ships at the ready | Atlantic Council
item.description: A combined Amphibious Ready Group and Marine Expeditionary Unit bring a tremendous capability, but only if there are enough ships available. The post The United States needs more amphibious ships at the ready appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Russia Building New Infrastructure For Major Troop Deployments Along NATO’s Northern Flank | The War Zone
item.description: As Russia builds facilities for tens of thousands of troops, NATO is also bolstering its defenses with a new Arctic combat force to act as a deterrent. The post Russia Building New Infrastructure For Major Troop Deployments Along NATO’s Northern Flank appeared first on The War…

item.title: 📍 The Aliens Aren’t the Most Unbelievable Part of Steven Spielberg’s New Movie | Foreign Policy
item.description: Would even extraterrestrials inspire humanity to unite these days?

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon may ‘sacrifice’ traditional weapons to buy more drones if reconciliation fails: CTO | Breaking Defense
item.description: “We just make other trade-offs, like against exquisite weapons and systems: How much of those are we willing to sacrifice in place of low-cost autonomous weapons,” Emil Michael said.

item.title: 🛡️ Somalia is the dark horse in US defense and economic strategy | Atlantic Council
item.description: US policymakers have long viewed Somalia primarily through a counterterrorism lens. But as major maritime supply routes come under strain, its position along key sea lanes is reshaping its geopolitical relevance—and external actors like Turkey are already moving to capitalize.…

item.title: 🏴 As the US targets Brazil’s payment system, Europe should pay close attention | Atlantic Council
item.description: In its determination on Brazil’s trade practices, the USTR has zeroed in on the instant payment system Pix—raising alarms not only in Brasília but also in Brussels. The post As the US targets Brazil’s payment system, Europe should pay close attention appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🕵️ Andrew Giuliani on the White House’s World Cup security playbook | Atlantic Council
item.description: The FIFA World Cup kicks off this week across the US, Mexico, and Canada, bringing with it millions of fans, dozens of teams, and what officials describe as a security challenge unlike any sporting event the United States has hosted before. The post Andrew Giuliani on the White…

item.title: 🏴 Russia, Land of the Unpredictable Past | Foreign Policy
item.description: A new censorship law restricts what Russians were ever allowed to have sung, written, or published.

item.title: 📍 Tiny drones making a buzz at the Berlin Air Show | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Breaking Defense team wraps up the last day of the Berlin Air Show.

item.title: 🌏 What in the World? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Test yourself on the week of June 6: Peru’s presidential race heads for a photo finish, Xi Jinping visits North Korea, and the World Cup kicks off.

item.title: 🏴 Germany And Spain Launch ‘Team Gen 6’ After Europe’s Next-Gen Fighter Effort Collapses | The War Zone
item.description: The emerging coalition could reshape Europe’s future fighter landscape, but it still needs political backing, lots of funding, and likely more partners. The post Germany And Spain Launch ‘Team Gen 6’ After Europe’s Next-Gen Fighter Effort Collapses appeared first on The War…

item.title: 📍 Trump Is Shattering the Illusion of the West | Foreign Policy
item.description: The president’s turn to imperial civilizationalism is destroying what it claims to defend.

item.title: 🌍 Kroenig interviewed on BBC on Iran negotiations | Atlantic Council
item.description: On June 11, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig was interviewed on BBC about ongoing negotiations with Iran. The post Kroenig interviewed on BBC on Iran negotiations appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 How to build energy resilience in an uncertain, contested world | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read the reflections of Atlantic Council experts on the 2026 Global Energy Forum that explored how the US, allies, and partners can fortify interconnections against the weaponization of energy. The post How to build energy resilience in an uncertain, contested world appeared…

item.title: 📍 Dispatch from Istanbul: Why Turkey is throwing its weight behind ‘zero waste’ ahead of COP31 | Atlantic Council
item.description: A recent gathering in Istanbul offered a preview of Ankara's priorities ahead of the United Nations climate conference later this year. The post Dispatch from Istanbul: Why Turkey is throwing its weight behind ‘zero waste’ ahead of COP31 appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 How Best to Look at Russian History | Foreign Policy
item.description: A new book shows the uses and limits of microhistories in understanding the past.

item.title: 🏛️ Boeing bows out of Navy’s new trainer jet competition | Breaking Defense
item.description: Boeing said the T-7A Red Hawk training jet they are producing for the Air Force doesn’t fulfill Navy requirements.

item.title: 🌍 Will Farmers Lose Faith With Trump? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The economic effects of the Iran war are straining a key Republican constituency ahead of midterm elections.

item.title: 🌏 Poland intends to buy two more squads of F-35s, minister says | Breaking Defense
item.description: Two extra squadrons of F-35s would double the existing order, from 32 to 64.

item.title: 🕵️ Better collaboration needed on SOCOM programs, says government watchdog | Breaking Defense
item.description: “We found that these [civilian] officials can’t effectively carry out the office’s oversight responsibilities as they can’t always get access to relevant program information or meetings,” GAO said while listing three recommendations.

item.title: 🏛️ Four U-2S Spy Planes Would Be Restored In Bill That Would Save The Dragon Lady Fleet | The War Zone
item.description: Congress is moving again to block the Air Force from retiring the U-2 amid continued questions about capability gaps. The post Four U-2S Spy Planes Would Be Restored In Bill That Would Save The Dragon Lady Fleet appeared first on The War Zone.
